body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 24/2026
Made under: Home Owner Grant Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: March 9, 2026

B.C. Reg. 24/2026, deposited March 9, 2026, under theHOME OWNER GRANT ACT[section 18 (2) and (3)]. Order in Council 69/2026, approved and ordered March 9, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective January 1, 2026, the Home Owner Grant Regulation, B.C. Reg. 100/2002, is amended in section 22 by striking out "$2 175 000" and substituting "$2 075 000". — B. BAILEY,Minister of Finance; M. FARNWORTH,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC home owner grant threshold drops to $2,075,000 for 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: The British Columbia government has lowered the property value threshold for the Home Owner Grant from $2,175,000 to $2,075,000, effective January 1, 2026. This means that homeowners whose property is assessed at more than $2,075,000 will no longer qualify for the grant, which reduces the amount of property tax they pay on their principal residence.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check your 2026 property assessment; if it exceeds $2,075,000, you will not receive the Home Owner Grant this year.

key_dates.label: BC Home Owner Grant lower threshold takes effect
